service.name: FinancialBenchmarkService
service.description:
Apache Axis2/C HTTP/2 Financial Benchmark Service
Demonstrates native C performance for financial calculations on hardware too constrained for Java/WildFly (old Android phones, embedded systems, edge devices).
Operations: - portfolioVariance: O(n²) covariance matrix calculation - monteCarlo: Compute-intensive VaR simulation - scenarioAnalysis: Hash table vs linear lookup comparison - metadata: Service information and device stats
Uses pure json-c library (no XML/SOAP dependencies)

operation.name: portfolioVariance
operation.parameter: Calculate portfolio variance using an O(n²) covariance matrix. Returns portfolio volatility and annualised volatility. Benchmark: 500 assets in <10ms on constrained hardware.

operation.name: monteCarlo
operation.parameter: Run a Monte Carlo VaR simulation using Geometric Brownian Motion. Compute-intensive: 10 000 sims × 252 periods = 2.52M iterations. Returns VaR 95/99, CVaR, max drawdown, and probability of profit.

operation.name: scenarioAnalysis
operation.parameter: Scenario-weighted portfolio analysis using O(1) hash lookups vs O(n) linear search. Returns expected portfolio value and volatility across probability-weighted price scenarios.

operation.name: metadata
operation.parameter: Get FinancialBenchmarkService metadata: supported operations, capacity limits, current memory usage, and device platform information. No request body required.

operation.name: generateTestData
operation.parameter: Generate a synthetic equal-weighted portfolio with a positive-semi-definite covariance matrix. Returns a ready-to-use portfolioVariance request body.
operation.description:
Generate synthetic portfolio test data for benchmarking.
Creates a valid portfolio request with: - Equal-weighted portfolio - Synthetic covariance matrix (positive semi-definite) - Realistic correlation structure
Example JSON Request: { "n_assets": 500 }
Returns a complete portfolioVariance request JSON.
